title: Duration in the Deep Past
description: Using Past Perfect Continuous to show how long something was happening before another past event.
lesson_content_markdown:
# Duration in the Deep Past

Sometimes we want to show **how long** an action continued before another past event. We use the **Past Perfect Continuous** for this.

### 1. The Timeline
Imagine you were studying for three hours, and then your friend arrived.
- **Event 1 (Long Action)**: I was studying for 3 hours.
- **Event 2 (Interruption)**: My friend arrived.
- **Combined**: *"I **had been studying** for three hours when my friend arrived."*

### 2. How to Make It
Use **had** + **been** + **V-ing**.

- **Formula**: Subject + had + been + doing word (ing)
- **Example**: She **had been working** at the university since 2018 when she was promoted.

### 3. Why use it?
It shows that an action was *in progress* and had a *duration* before something else happened in the past.

- **Past Continuous**: I **was studying** when the phone rang. (One moment is focus.)
- **Past Perfect Continuous**: I **had been studying** for two hours when the phone rang. (The **length of time** is focus.)

### 4. Common Keywords
- **For**: I had been waiting **for** 30 minutes.
- **Since**: They had been playing **since** noon.

---

mastery_check.prompt_to_student: Can you show the duration of actions in the deep past?

questions.question: He ___ for two hours when the professor finally arrived.
They ___ since 9:00 AM before the rain started.
She ___ on her computer for three hours before it crashed.
Which sentence shows a deep past duration?
options: I had been studying for three hours when the light went out.
I have been studying for three hours when the light went out.
I was studying for three hours when the light had gone out.
I am studying since three hours when the light go out.
questions.correct_answer: I had been studying for three hours when the light went out.

questions.question: We ___ for the bus in the cold for twenty minutes.
By the time he finished university, he ___ in that lab for four years.
The professor ___ for 20 minutes when the first student walked in.
My friends ___ before the game was cancelled.
I ___ all day, so I was very hungry at dinner.
Which question asks about duration in the deep past?
options: How long had you been waiting before he called?
How much were you waiting before he called?
How long have you been waiting before he called?
How often had you being waiting before he called?
questions.correct_answer: How long had you been waiting before he called?

questions.question: I ___ (study) for three hours when my computer died.
He ___ (wait) for the professor since noon when he finally saw her.
They ___ (walk) through the campus for thirty minutes before it rained.
___ the professor (give) instructions for a long time when you arrived?
We ___ (use) the same laboratory since last summer.
She ___ (try) to open the locked door for ten minutes.
My roommate ___ (not listen) to the radio when I came in.
It ___ (snow) for two hours when the university closed.
How long ___ you (look) for your phone before you found it?
They ___ (prepare) for the campus party all day Saturday.
I *have been studying* for two hours when the light went out.
He *were waiting* for since noon before she arrived.
They *had being training* for months before the final game.
We had *be walking* for five miles before we found the library.
She *has been working* at that university for ten years before she quit.
It *was rained* since three o'clock before the picnic began.
How long *was you been looking* for that book?
They *have been playing* football for an hour when it began to rain.
I *had be eating* my lunch when the professor called my name.
Everything *had being going* well until the last minute.
